hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-specific TM6 (272/273/276/279) and reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved, but selectivity is compromised by conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Targeted mutagenesis at these positions is necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To specifically target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely occurring.
Introduce mutations at focus positions to disrupt off-target interactions while maintaining native-like binding to SSTR2.
